blocks.text:
# SPRING GLOW (SGX) PROGRAM — SPEND & RISK AUDIT MEMO

**Scope:** All Northstar Media Pty Ltd departments touching the Spring Glow Campaign Execution (SGX) program — Campaign, Finance, Legal, HR, Operations, IT.
**Method:** Cross-referenced every SGX-tagged document retrievable in the knowledge base. Note up front: this program's paper trail is unusually fragmented — multiple documents carry near-identical bodies but differ by exactly one calendar year in date/invoice numbering (a pattern flagged throughout as a data-integrity concern, see §5).

---

## 1. TOTAL PROGRAM COST — ALL DEPARTMENTS

No single document reconciles all six departments. Five different "total" figures exist in the record (detail and sourcing in §4). After reconciling, my constructed cross-department total is:

**≈ $566,000–$575,000 AUD**, built as follows:
- $534,833 (floor) — cross-departmental budget spanning Marketing/Operations/Technology/Personnel, `budget_1750_589.md` / `budget_1750_589.csv` (file 346/1181) and its variant `budget_1750_589_v1.csv` (351): Skyline Analytics Data Analytics $158,333 (v1: $158,334), EcoPackaging Australia Sustainable Packaging $8,500, Campaign Material Distribution $15,000, CRM System Upgrade $48,000, Department Staff Salaries $210,000, Advertising $95,000.
- **+ $15,650** Legal costs not captured in the above sheet — Red Centre Legal $8,450 + Dingo Consulting $7,200, `invoice_0203_288.md` (260) / `_v1.txt` (495).
- **+ $15,800** Kangaroo Designs creative-video cost tagged directly "Project: SGX" in `budget_0446_702.md` (359), which does not appear in either master budget sheet.

**Why this base is the most authoritative:** `budget_1750_589` is the *only* SGX document that itemizes cost across Marketing, Operations, Technology, **and** Personnel simultaneously (i.e., actually attempts a cross-department view rather than a Campaign-only creative/media budget), it tracks per-line **Approved/Pending** status, and it is the sheet implicitly required by the Legal/Campaign spend-tracking policy (`policy_0615_666_v1.md`, file 499, "SGX-Spend-Track-2026," mandating weekly cross-verification of spend against budget). The four other "total" documents (below) are Campaign-only creative/media estimates authored at different points in time by different people, none of which reference Legal, HR, Operations, or IT costs at all — meaning they **understate** true program cost by definition.

**Key caveat:** the evidence tool could not confirm `budget_1750_589` has no rows beyond those retrieved, so $534,833 is a confirmed *floor*, not a verified ceiling. Treat the reconciled total as directional, not final — this itself is an audit finding (no department produced a genuinely complete, all-cost total).

---

## 2. RISK REGISTER

| # | Item | Dept | Amount | Status/Flag | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Red Centre Legal — legal review/compliance audit | Legal | $8,450.00 | **Unpaid ("Amount Due")**; billed under **two conflicting invoice numbers** for the identical service/date (NMS-INV-2026-05-07-012 vs NMS-INV-2027-05-07-012, both dated 2026-05-07, same author) → duplicate-payment risk | `invoice_0203_288.md` (260), `invoice_0203_288_v1.txt` (495) |
| 2 | Dingo Consulting — market research/audience segmentation | Legal | $7,200.00 | Same invoice pair as #1 — unpaid, duplicate-numbering risk | `invoice_0203_288.md` / `_v1.txt` |
| 3 | External legal retainer (IP, brand protection, compliance) | Legal | $7,500.00/month (≥$45,000 over 6-month min. term) | **Unsigned — "subject to final legal department approval"** | `memo_0568_213.txt` (328), `_v1.txt` (812) |
| 4 | Vendor due-diligence allocation tied to Red Centre Legal SLA gap | Legal | $15,000.00 | Additional funds allocated after an **SLA/data-handling gap flagged, breach not confirmed** — open item | `status_report_0233_459.md` (1176) |
| 5 | NorthPac Legal line item | Legal/Campaign | $7,200.00 | Project tag truncated in source (SGX not fully confirmed); **identical dollar amount to Dingo Consulting invoice #2** — coincidence warrants independent verification against possible double-billing | `budget_0446_702.md` (359) |
| 6 | Kangaroo Designs — creative video (tagline "Echo your journey") | Campaign | $15,800.00 | Confirmed Project=SGX, but **absent from both master SGX budgets** (`budget_1720_421`, `budget_1750_589`) → unbudgeted/untracked spend | `budget_0446_702.md` (359) |
| 7 | Department Staff Salaries / Advertising lines | Campaign/Finance | $210,000 / $95,000 | Document summary explicitly notes multiple line items **"Pending approval"** — approval status unresolved as of document date | `budget_1750_589.md` (346) |
| 8 | IT support for TVC packshot rendering discrepancy (TechFlow IT) | IT/Campaign | Not costed anywhere | **Control gap**: work explicitly performed and a creative-asset hold ordered, but no invoice/budget line exists for it in any SGX document | `memo_0764_205.txt` (727) / `_v1.txt` (401) |
| 9 | Zone C evening-shift staffing increase (5→6 staff/shift) tied to SGX demand | Operations | Not costed | **Control gap** — incremental headcount explicitly attributed to SGX with no dollar figure tracked against any SGX budget | `policy_0594_948.md` (944) / `_v1.md` (1733) |
| 10 | Staffing/roster discrepancies (Operations 14 reported vs 16 scheduled; Accounting 9 vs 12) on 2026-05-07 | Operations/Finance | Unquantified | **Flagged non-compliant by Legal**; all departments ordered to resubmit staffing templates by 2026-06-25 | `memo_0533_432.md` (798) / `_v1.txt` (791) |
| 11 | HiringMinds HR — contract renewal lag | HR | Unquantified | Flagged in Legal's vendor compliance review as "lagging on renewals" — lapse risk for an SGX-adjacent recruiting vendor | `status_report_0233_459.md` (1176), `vendor_review_1737_532.md` (285) |
| 12 | Systemic duplicate-document pattern | All | N/A (affects every $ above) | At least 7 SGX-related document pairs are near-identical in body text but dated **exactly one calendar year apart** (see §5) — indicates either an uncontrolled document-duplication defect or genuine re-submission/duplicate-billing exposure | multiple, listed §5 |

---

## 3. VENDOR CROSS-REFERENCE

**Campaign's own SGX vendor set** (from `budget_1720_421`, `budget_0355_340`, `budget_1750_589`, `budget_0446_702`): Aurora Research Pty Ltd, Melbournescope Studios, Skyline Analytics, EcoPackaging Australia, Kangaroo Designs, Red Centre Legal, Dingo Consulting, NorthPac Legal.

**Vendors appearing in other departments' SGX records but absent from Campaign's own vendor list:**
| Vendor | Dept it appears in | Source | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sydney Events Pty Ltd | Campaign project-status (Felix Carmichael) | `project_update_1420_449.md` (141) | Named as having "met milestones" for SGX; no budget line anywhere |
| Perth Tech Solutions | Campaign project-status | `project_update_1420_449.md` (141) | Same — no corresponding invoice/budget entry |
| SouthCoast Logistics | Campaign/Operations status report | `status_report_0352_320.txt` (596) | Delivered SGX creative assets; not in any budget sheet |
| Southern Logistics Pty Ltd | Campaign/Operations status report | `status_report_0352_320.txt` (596) | Distributing SGX printed materials; not in any budget sheet |
| TechFlow IT | IT/Campaign coordination memo | `memo_0764_205.txt` (727) | Performing paid rendering-software investigation; no invoice/budget presence |
| HiringMinds HR | Legal vendor-compliance review | `status_report_0233_459.md` (1176) | Facilitates SGX-adjacent recruiting; absent from Campaign vendor list |

**Vendors whose billed amount differs across records:**
- **Skyline Analytics:** $158,333 in `budget_1750_589.md`/`.csv` vs **$158,334** in `budget_1750_589_v1.csv` (351) — $1 inter-version discrepancy.
- **Dingo Consulting ($7,200) vs NorthPac Legal ($7,200):** two different legal/consulting vendors billing an identical figure under separate invoices for SGX-adjacent legal work — flagged for independent verification, not confirmed as duplicate, but statistically improbable coincidence.

---

## 4. PROGRAM-TOTAL VARIANCE (SHOW YOUR WORK)

All candidate "total" figures located, in order of discovery:

| Candidate Total | Source Document | Author / Date | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| $54,200 | `spring_glow_budget.csv` (343) | No author/date/dept given | Sum of media $25,000 + production $15,000 + agency $8,000 + talent $5,000 + printing $1,200. No "SGX" tag; likely a partial/legacy creative-only sheet |
| $385,000 | `memo_0778_234.txt` (408) / `_v1.md` (384) | Marco Nguyen, Campaign Manager; 2026-02-07 / 2027-02-07 | Creative production $120,000 + Digital media $150,000 + Audience research/analytics $115,000 |
| $435,000 | `budget_1720_421.txt` (364) / `_v1.md` (350) / `.md` (103) | Sarah Chen; prepared 2026-01-28 | Campaign Mgmt salaries $120,000–$121,000 (inconsistent between copies) + Content Creation $60,000 + Production $70,000 + Digital advertising $60,000 + Creative services $45,000 + unspecified activation/contingency |
| $525,000 budgeted / **$525,600 actual** | `budget_0355_340.txt` (127) / `_v1.md` (367) | Quinn Reyes, Media Planner; 2025-10-01 / 2026-10-01 | +0.1% variance; Creative Production over budget, Media Buying/Legal & Compliance near/under |
| **$534,833 (floor)** | `budget_1750_589.md`/`.csv` (346/1181) / `_v1.csv` (351) | Cross-department (Marketing/Ops/Tech/Personnel) | Only document spanning all functional areas; likely incomplete (more rows may exist) |

**Highest figure found:** $534,833 (`budget_1750_589`)
**Lowest figure found:** $54,200 (`spring_glow_budget.csv`)

**Variance = $534,833 − $54,200 = $480,633**
**As % of highest = $480,633 / $534,833 = 89.9%**

(Sensitivity check using the next-largest fully-closed total, $525,600 actual spend, instead of the uncertain floor: gap = $471,400, = 89.7% of highest — materially the same conclusion: an ~90% spread between the lowest and highest total ever reported for the same program.)

---

## 5. PERSON CROSS-REFERENCE (SIGNERS / SENDERS / APPROVERS)

| Name | Title | Dept | Doc(s) | Date(s) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Marco Nguyen | Campaign Manager | Campaign | `memo_0778_234`/`_v1`, `policy_0615_666_v1` | 2026-02-07/2027-02-07; 2027-06-14 |
| Paulo Eriksen | Campaign Manager | Campaign | `memo_1954_815` | 2026-04-15 |
| Freya Patel | Campaign Manager | Campaign | `status_report_0352_320`, `email_0795_683` | 2025-11-15; 2025-09-28 |
| Paulo Silva | Brand Strategist | Campaign | `memo_0764_205`/`_v1` | 2025-08-10/2026-08-10 |
| Isla Carmichael | Brand Strategist | Campaign | `invoice_0203_288`/`_v1` | 2026-05-07 (both) |
| Felix Carmichael | Media Planner | Campaign | `project_update_1420_449` | 2025-12-13 |
| Quinn Reyes | Media Planner | Campaign | `budget_0355_340`/`_v1`, `meeting_notes_1060_435` | 2025-10-01/2026-10-01; 2026 |
| Sarah Chen | (title not stated) | Campaign (implied) | `budget_1720_421` | 2026-01-28 |
| Hugo Johansson | Legal Counsel | Legal | `status_report_0233_459` | 2026-01-23 |
| Freya Walsh | Paralegal | Legal | `memo_0568_213`/`_v1` | 2025-08-02/2026-08-02 |
| Henry Duarte | IT Manager | IT | `status_report_0997_389`/`_v1` | 2025-08-04/2026 |
| Freya Farrell | Site Supervisor | Operations | `roster_1310_811` | 2025-07-22 |

**Flags:**
1. **"Campaign Manager" title held by three different named individuals** (Marco Nguyen, Paulo Eriksen, Freya Patel) across overlapping SGX date ranges (Feb–Apr 2026), with no org-chart or handover document explaining succession — a data-integrity/possible-fraud flag per the instructions (inconsistent role occupancy for the same title on the same program).
2. **Repeating first name "Freya" across three different departments and titles** — Freya Patel (Campaign Manager), Freya Walsh (Paralegal, Legal), Freya Farrell (Site Supervisor, Operations). Combined with the "Paulo" repeat (Paulo Silva/Paulo Eriksen, both Campaign, different titles) and the shared surname "Carmichael" (Isla Carmichael/Felix Carmichael, both Campaign, different first names/titles), this naming pattern looks templated rather than organic and should be independently verified against HR headcount records.
3. **Systemic paired-document dating pattern** — the following document pairs have essentially identical bodies but are dated exactly one calendar year apart (or, in one case, differ only in invoice number):
   - `memo_0764_205.txt` (2025-08-10) / `_v1.txt` (2026-08-10)
   - `invoice_0203_288.md` (invoice # …2026-05-07-012) / `_v1.txt` (invoice # …2027-05-07-012, same body date 2026-05-07)
   - `budget_0355_340.txt` (2025-10-01) / `_v1.md` (2026-10-01)
   - `memo_0778_234.txt` (2026-02-07) / `_v1.md` (2027-02-07)
   - `memo_0568_213.txt` (2025-08-02) / `_v1.txt` (2026-08-02)
   - `memo_0533_432.md` / `_v1.txt` (2026 vs 2027)
   - `status_report_0997_389.txt` (2025) / `_v1.md` (2026)

   This pattern — same signer, same content, same dollar amounts, invoice/document numbers off by exactly one year — is the single highest-priority finding in this audit: it is consistent with either (a) an uncontrolled duplication defect in document management, or (b) genuine duplicate submission/re-billing. Given invoice #1 in the risk register ($8,450 + $7,200 = $15,650) is an actual "Amount Due" pair with mismatched invoice numbers on the same service date, **Finance should treat this as an active duplicate-payment risk requiring reconciliation before any payment is released.**

---

## 6. SOURCE INDEX (all figures/claims cited above)

- `spring_glow_budget.csv` (343) — $54,200 sum
- `budget_1720_421.txt` (364), `_v1.md` (350), `.md` (103) — $435,000 total; Sarah Chen
- `budget_0355_340.txt` (127), `_v1.md` (367) — $525,000/$525,600; Quinn Reyes
- `memo_0778_234.txt` (408), `_v1.md` (384) — $385,000; Marco Nguyen
- `budget_1750_589.md` (346), `.csv` (1181), `_v1.csv` (351) — $534,833 floor
- `budget_0446_702.md` (359) — Kangaroo Designs $15,800, NorthPac Legal $7,200
- `invoice_0203_288.md` (260), `_v1.txt` (495) — Red Centre Legal $8,450 + Dingo Consulting $7,200
- `memo_0568_213.txt` (328), `_v1.txt` (812) — $7,500/month retainer, pending approval
- `status_report_0233_459.md` (1176) — $15,000 due-diligence allocation; HiringMinds HR renewal lag
- `memo_0764_205.txt` (727), `_v1.txt` (401) — TechFlow IT rendering-discrepancy hold
- `policy_0594_948.md` (944), `_v1.md` (1733) — Zone C staffing increase
- `memo_0533_432.md` (798), `_v1.txt` (791) — staffing/roster discrepancy, non-compliance flag
- `policy_0615_666_v1.md` (499) — SGX-Spend-Track policy; Marco Nguyen
- `project_update_1420_449.md` (141) — Sydney Events Pty Ltd, Perth Tech Solutions; Felix Carmichael
- `status_report_0352_320.txt` (596) — SouthCoast/Southern Logistics; Freya Patel
- `status_report_0997_389.txt` (162), `_v1.md` (167) — Henry Duarte, IT Manager
- `roster_1310_811.md` (613) — Freya Farrell, Site Supervisor (LOG project, SGX-adjacent)
- `meeting_notes_1060_435.txt` (1115) — Quinn Reyes, SGX status (85% media buy complete)
- `vendor_review_1737_532.md` (285) — Red Centre Legal 4.5/5, HiringMinds HR 4/5, SGX kickoff 2025-07-15

**Bottom line:** No department produced a complete, verifiable, all-in SGX total. The best reconciled estimate is **~$566,000 AUD**, against a documented spread of **$54,200–$534,833 (a $480,633 / 89.9% gap)** between the lowest and highest figures ever put on paper for the same program — compounded by duplicate invoicing exposure (~$15,650 minimum) and a suspicious pattern of near-identical, year-shifted documents and repeating personnel names that warrants a formal forensic follow-up before any outstanding SGX invoice is paid.
